refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple cycle phases (load, travel, dump) into a single requirement, which implies multiple obligations. To be atomic and solution-neutral, it is necessary to separate the cycle time targets for each phase to clearly define what the system must achieve without implying how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for each individual phase (load, travel, dump) under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, and verify that the 95th percentile cycle time (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target for that phase.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load cycle.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel cycle.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load cycle sequencing and timing
Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback
Design load cycle with modular task decomposition to parallelize operations
Incorporate buffer and staging areas to smooth load cycle variability
Use predictive scheduling to anticipate and mitigate delays
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback

potential_sources: Dependency on accurate and timely feedback signals
Integration with cycle time measurement and control subsystems
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on adaptive control which logically isolates cycle time management from other system functions, supporting independence. Potential coupling arises from reliance on feedback data, but this is a necessary and manageable interface.

assumptions_risks: Real-time feedback data is accurate and available under all operational conditions
Control algorithms can respond quickly enough to meet cycle time targets
Operational policies and safety plans do not impose constraints that prevent adaptive control implementation
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the feedback and control interface as a separate DP or IM to further isolate dependencies and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for overall system control and adaptive cycle time management

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time feedback data for adaptive control

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated cycle time commands to execution modules

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive cycle time control is a core control function; minimal diffusion as it mainly interacts via telemetry and tasking interfaces.
Vehicle module is more about physical actuation; adaptive control logic would be diffused across vehicle subsystems.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control; adaptive cycle time control is more autonomous and local, causing diffusion.
Safety services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not cycle time control, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing adaptive cycle time control because it centralizes control logic and manages real-time feedback, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data which may be affected by network latency.
If adaptive control logic grows complex, it may require further decomposition to avoid a monolithic NGHE Solution module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks increase, consider refining the DP to separate feedback processing and control decision-making into distinct DPs to allow clearer modularization within NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback' directly addresses the FR of supporting the customer-defined cycle time target by adapting control based on live data, which aligns with the acceptance criterion of achieving p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target under specified conditions.

diffusion_risks: Adaptive cycle time control logic could become diffused if it grows complex and is not properly decomposed within the NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion (containment score 8) and low diffusion (diffusion score 2), indicating good containment of the DP. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring the cycle time performance (p95 ≤ CT_target) under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, and ensuring the system operates within the worksite safety plan and operational policies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear, as it centralizes adaptive control logic and manages real-time feedback through defined interfaces (Telemetry in, Tasking out).

recommended_revisions: Monitor the complexity of the adaptive control logic within the NGHE Solution module; if it grows, consider decomposing the DP into separate functions such as feedback processing and control decision-making to improve modularity and reduce diffusion risk.
Ensure robust handling of telemetry data latency and accuracy to mitigate dependency risks.
Periodically review module containment and diffusion scores to detect any emerging diffusion issues early.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and properly contained within the implementation module. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and containment and diffusion scores support the current module boundary choice. Although there are recommendations to monitor complexity and data handling risks, these do not necessitate further decomposition at this time, especially given the high safety criticality which favors stability and clarity in design.

revise.why: Monitor the complexity of the adaptive control logic within the NGHE Solution module; if complexity grows, consider decomposing the DP into sub-functions to improve modularity and reduce diffusion risk.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module will maintain high cohesion and low diffusion as currently measured.
Telemetry data latency and accuracy will be managed effectively within the module.
open_risks: Potential diffusion of adaptive control logic if complexity increases.
Telemetry data latency and accuracy impacting control performance.
evidence_needed: Periodic containment and diffusion score reviews.
Performance data verifying p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target under specified conditions.
Verification of compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
